newsSinceJun6: No new fundamental news since June 6; the material event remains the June 3 Q2 FY26 print (record $10.8B AI semi revenue +143% y/y, EPS beat) where the unraised $56B FY26 AI guide and a $16B Q3 AI guide vs ~$17.2B consensus triggered a ~13%/$280B single-day wipeout. Since June 6 the action is continued flow-driven de-rating (-2.96% today, ~20% off highs, YTD compressed to +10.7%) while sell-side defends with raised targets (BofA $530, Jefferies $550, consensus ~$501) and management's FY27 $100B+ AI revenue and multi-year Google/Anthropic/OpenAI/Meta compute deals stay intact.

sources.label: CNBC - Broadcom plunges on weak software, unchanged AI chip forecast (Jun 3, 2026)
Yahoo Finance - Broadcom's sell-off just entered megacap history
Motley Fool - Down 20%, should you buy Broadcom on the dip? (Jun 7, 2026)
TheStreet - Bank of America resets Broadcom price target after earnings
StockAnalysis - AVGO analyst consensus and price targets

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday already moved it down for crowding and distance from the passive/power bottleneck stack; the continued post-earnings flush improves entry slightly but a $1.8T benchmark mega-cap cannot become hidden or under-owned, so no second move.
verdicts.bull: A ~20% drawdown against a reaffirmed $56B FY26 / $100B+ FY27 AI revenue roadmap restores some asymmetry even for a benchmark name.
verdicts.neutral: AVGO stays a monitor/benchmark proxy on this list; its de-rating matters more as a signal for the smaller passive/power names than as alpha itself.
verdicts.bear: Guidance that merely met its own prior bar at peak expectations shows how little upside surprise is left in the most-owned AI names.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY27 hyperscaler deployment ramp toward the $100B+ AI revenue target
verdicts.primaryRisk: Index/passive crowding means any AI capex digestion hits AVGO first and hardest

verdicts.rankRationale: On a node-relevance lens the thesis is fully intact (custom ASIC + scale-up fabric revenue accelerating to +200% y/y in Q3) while the price de-rated ~20% and YTD compressed to +10.7% versus the 14.1% yesterday's hold was anchored on; sell-off with thesis intact improves asymmetry.
verdicts.bull: Q3 AI guide of $16B (+200% y/y) and reaffirmed $100B+ FY27 with Google/Anthropic/OpenAI/Meta contracts confirm AVGO as the central custom-silicon node, now ~30% below consensus price targets.
verdicts.neutral: The expectations reset was about Wall Street's bar, not Broadcom's roadmap; rank improvement is bounded by mega-cap scale and visibility.
verdicts.bear: Soft infrastructure software and an unraised FY26 AI guide hint that even the strongest node can disappoint a market pricing perpetual upside surprises.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Fiscal 2027 multi-gigawatt custom accelerator deployments beginning to ship
verdicts.primaryRisk: Customer concentration: a single hyperscaler pausing or insourcing its ASIC program

verdicts.rankRationale: The page's own thesis concedes AVGO's CPO alpha is 'not undiscovered'; the post-earnings flush improves the entry marginally but does nothing to make the consensus ecosystem anchor under-owned versus smaller CPO pure-plays.
verdicts.bull: CPO platform leadership plus accelerating AI networking revenue at a 20%-cheaper price modestly improves risk/reward.
verdicts.neutral: AVGO anchors the CPO ecosystem either way; the alpha on this list sits with suppliers into its platform, not the platform itself.
verdicts.bear: If hyperscaler networking budgets digest after the FY26 ramp, the most-owned name in the category de-rates further before the pure-plays do.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Large-cap valuation still embeds flawless multi-year AI networking share retention

verdicts.rankRationale: Already rank 1 on the silicon-consumption theme and the quarter validated it: AI silicon consumption accelerating from $10.8B to a guided $16B per quarter (+200% y/y) keeps it the cleanest basket proxy; the de-rating changes price, not theme leadership.
verdicts.bull: Both legs of the thesis (custom accelerators and AI networking/interconnect) posted record, accelerating revenue with multi-year hyperscaler contracts behind them.
verdicts.neutral: Rank 1 is retained on relevance; the 20% drawdown is an expectations event, not a change in silicon consumption trajectory.
verdicts.bear: The sell-off is a warning that silicon-consumption proxies are priced for guidance raises, not reaffirmations.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY27 $100B+ AI revenue trajectory confirming compute-consumption growth
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI capex digestion in 2027 would hit the consumption-proxy thesis directly

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down was explicitly predicated on a '39.8% YTD' rerating that no longer exists - YTD is now +10.7% and the stock is ~20% off highs with the optics/networking thesis intact and accelerating, so the setup genuinely changed and asymmetry improved.
verdicts.bull: AI networking and 800G/1.6T optical attach revenue is accelerating into FY27 deployments while the rerating penalty that justified the prior demotion has been flushed out.
verdicts.neutral: Still a mega-cap with broad ownership; the move up corrects a stale-rerating penalty rather than claiming hidden alpha.
verdicts.bear: Software softness and the unraised FY26 guide could mark the start of a longer expectations reset rather than a one-quarter flush.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Scale-out networking and CPO content growth in fiscal 2027 hyperscaler builds
verdicts.primaryRisk: Ethernet/optics pricing pressure if hyperscalers dual-source interconnect
